pom.xml.general_idea: Update Spring Boot parent version to a release compatible with Java 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.4.x does not support Java 17; compilation or runtime failures occur due to internal API changes in JDK 17 (e.g., module encapsulation).

pom.xml.general_idea: Configure the project to compile and run against Java 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this property, Maven defaults to the previous Java version (11), causing compilation errors if Java 17 features are used or if the build toolchain is set to 17.

src/main/resources/application.propertie.general_idea: Configure Spring MVC to use the ANT_PATH_MATCHER strategy to avoid deprecation warnings and runtime errors in Spring Boot 2.6+ (which aligns with Java 17 support).
src/main/resources/application.propertie.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.6+ changed the default path matching strategy from AntPathMatcher to PathPatternParser. Without this explicit configuration, applications using certain features (like Swagger/Springfox or specific path matching logic) may fail to start or behave incorrectly due to the deprecated default.
